data.narrative:
Twenty is a Series A defense and security company founded by Joe Lin, former Palo Alto Networks VP who led the Expanse acquisition integration. The company has raised $38M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI, all at Series A stage in the Defense & Security sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $38M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implies a $76M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range from $125M (NODA AI) to $500M (Stark Defence). The peer-median method carries greater weight given direct stage and sector alignment, though the wide peer range ($125M–$500M) reflects heterogeneity in the cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $259M, with a median of $150M. Key uncertainties include Twenty's revenue traction and customer concentration relative to the peer set, the strategic premium attributable to Lin's background in high-value acquisitions, and market timing within the current defense technology funding environment. The peer range width suggests material differentiation among Series A defense companies by contract pipeline and technical capability.
